title: Orange and almond cake
subtitle: Whole boiled orange cake with ground almonds, gluten-free
excerpt: The whole orange is boiled until completely soft, then blended skin and all into the batter. This gives the cake an intense orange flavour with no bitterness — the boiling removes the harshness from the pith. The cake is naturally gluten-free.
sourceNotes: The whole-boiled-citrus cake is a Sephardic Jewish method, specifically the Passover orange and almond cake documented in Claudia Roden's A Book of Jewish Food (1996), which brought the recipe into mainstream British cooking. The technique of boiling the whole fruit is widely documented across Mediterranean Jewish cookery and Passover baking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze sliced and wrapped. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: This cake is better the day after baking. Store covered at room temperature for up to 3 days.

glossaryTerms.definition: A technique in which a whole unpeeled orange is submerged in cold water and simmered for 1–2 hours until completely soft throughout, including the pith and skin. The long, slow boil breaks down the bitter compounds in the pith and makes the entire orange — including the skin — suitable for blending directly into a cake batter without any sharpness.

content.text: produces a cake with a more intense flavour than one made with zest alone — the entire fruit goes into the batter, skin included. The boiling must be complete: press the orange with a spoon; it should offer no resistance at all before you drain it.

Place the {{orange}} in a saucepan, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil. Simmer for 1½–2 hours, keeping the oranges submerged, until completely soft when pressed. Drain and leave to cool completely. Cut each orange open, remove any pips, then blend the flesh, skin, and all to a smooth purée. This can be done the day before.

Heat the oven to 170°C (150°C fan). Grease and line a 23 cm round tin. Beat the {{eggs}} and {{caster-sugar}} together for 2 minutes until light. Stir in the orange purée. Fold in {{almond-flour}} and {{baking-powder}}. Pour into the tin and bake for 35–40 minutes until set across the surface and a skewer inserted 3 cm from the edge comes out clean — the centre will be slightly denser. Cool completely in the tin before turning out. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} to serve.
